title: GetSkillsManifest
description: Commands like `specd skills list` and `specd skills update` need to know which skills are currently installed per agent without loading the full validated config. `GetSkillsManifest` provides this by reading the `skills` section of `specd.yaml` via the `ConfigWriter` port's `readSkillsManifest` operation and returning a typed map of agent names to skill name arrays.
context: Commands like `specd skills list` and `specd skills update` need to know which skills are currently installed per agent without loading the full validated config. `GetSkillsManifest` provides this by reading the `skills` section of `specd.yaml` via the `ConfigWriter` port's `readSkillsManifest` operation and returning a typed map of agent names to skill name arrays.

rules:
`execute(input)` MUST accept a `GetSkillsManifestInput` object with the following field:

- `configPath` (string, required) — absolute path to the `specd.yaml` to read.
The use case MUST call `ConfigWriter.readSkillsManifest(configPath)` with the path from the input object. It SHALL NOT read or parse `specd.yaml` directly.

rules.requirement: Returns a map of agent to skill names
rules: `execute` MUST return `Promise<Record<string, string[]>>`. Each key is an agent name (e.g. `"claude"`); each value is an array of installed skill names for that agent.

rules.requirement: Returns empty object when no skills section exists
rules: When `specd.yaml` has no `skills` section, `readSkillsManifest` MUST return `{}` (an empty record). The use case MUST NOT treat absence as an error.

constraints: The use case is a thin pass-through to the ConfigWriter port.
GetSkillsManifest is constructed with a single dependency: a ConfigWriter instance.
The use case is async — it returns Promise<Record<string, string[]>>.
This is a targeted read that does not require a fully validated SpecdConfig — it reads only the skills key.

scenarios.name: Config path provided
then: the call succeeds and the path is forwarded to ConfigWriter.readSkillsManifest

scenarios.name: Port receives the config path
then: AND the use case performs no other I/O

scenarios.requirement: Returns a map of agent to skill names
scenarios.name: Multiple agents with skills

scenarios.requirement: Returns empty object when no skills section exists
scenarios.name: No skills section in config

scenarios.requirement: Returns empty object when no skills section exists
scenarios.name: Skills section exists but is empty
